exercises.name: Glute Side Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, isolate the movement to your hip as best as possible. 

2) Start in 4-point position. Hands underneath shoulder, knees underneath hips. 

3) Leg comes out to the side and draw a large circle keeping your tummy tight. 

4) Then switch direction. 

5) Alternate to the other side.

exercises.name: Glute-Ham Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start with feet placed in the foot pads of a GHD, and knees resting on the large pad. 

2. With the upper body in a straight line, and hips locked, bend forward at the knees until your body is parallel to the ground. 

3. In one motion, bring the whole upper body and the hips back up to the start position, with knees directly under shoulders.

exercises.name: Goblet Slider Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core engaged. Load up your back leg as much as you can and keep your legs at about 90 degrees at outer segments.

2.You’ll also wanna make sure your front knee doesn’t bend over top of your front toes.

3.With your feet on the sliders, carry kettlebells one on each hand. This will be your starting position.

4.Slowly extend one foot backwards until knee is close to the ground and the other knee is at 90 degrees. Then pull yourself back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

6.When you complete one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Goblet Stepup
exercises.description: Goblet Stepup

exercises.name: Goose Step
exercises.description:
1) When performing this mobility drill and warm up exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knees stay straight.

2) Step forward by lifting your leg from your hip flexor. Keep your leg straight, by keeping your quadriceps active. 

3) Lean forward, and step onto your front foot. Return your feet together. 

4) Alternate by stepping with your other leg. 

5) Repeat until prescribed reps or distance are completed.

exercises.name: Groiner
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility warm up, ensure that you allow your hips to drop down and open up towards the ground each stride you make. This will help stretch out your hips better.

2. Begin with both hands palms on the ground. Right leg stretched out and the left leg bent close to your left hand. This will be your starting position.

3. Using both legs, jump and switch up the positions of your leg. Left leg stretched out and right leg bent close to the right hand. 

4. Repeat.

exercises.name: Gymleco 082 Belt Squat Machine is your go-to for heavy lower body training #gymleco #gymleco #shorts
exercises.description: Gymleco 082 Belt Squat Machine is your go-to for heavy lower body training #gymleco #gymleco #shorts

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.description:
1. Start standing with the weight on your shoulders, and feet shoulder-width apart. 

2. Slowly lower down into a squat position, while keeping the lower back in contact with the pad. 

3. Go as low as you can comfortably go under control before returning to the start position.

exercises.name: Hack Squat | Exercise Guide
exercises.description: Hack Squat | Exercise Guide

exercises.name: Half Burpee with Dumbbell
exercises.description:
Start standing with dumbbells in hand. 

Lower into a squat, place the weights on the ground, and jump your feet back into a plank. 

Perform a push-up, jump your feet back in, then stand and press the dumbbells overhead. 

Repeat quickly.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling Band Overhead Press
exercises.description:
Kneel with one knee down, holding a resistance band anchored low. 

Press overhead with both hands.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling Band Pallof Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Kneel on one knee, facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handle in both hands. 

3. Make sure the knee that is up is the one closest to the anchor point.  

4. Press the handle directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling Band Pallof Press and Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Kneel on one knee, facing perpendicular to the band, holding the handle in both hands. 

3. Make sure the knee that is up is the one closest to the anchor point.  

4. Press the handle directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. With arms extended, bring the handle directly overhead.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling Single Arm Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
Start in a half-kneeling position with your back knee on the floor and the opposite foot planted in front. 

Hold a dumbbell in the hand on the same side as the back leg at shoulder height, palm facing forward. Keep your core tight and torso upright. 

Press the dumbbell straight up until your arm is fully extended overhead, then slowly lower it back to shoulder height.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling SuperBand Pallof Press
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Kneel on one knee, facing perpendicular to the band, holding the band in both hands. 

3. Make sure the knee that is up is the one closest to the anchor point.  

4. Press the band directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling SuperBand Pallof Press and Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Kneel on one knee, facing perpendicular to the band, holding the band in both hands. 

3. Make sure the knee that is up is the one closest to the anchor point.  

4. Press the band directly outwards and away from your body without letting your torso rotate. With arms extended, bring the band directly overhead.

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Half Kneeling SuperBand Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about hip height. 

2. Kneel on one knee, facing the anchor point, holding the band in one hand. 
 
3. Pull the band towards your armpit while keeping an open chest position. Be sure not to rotate the body as you pull. 

5. Slowly return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Half Lord of the Fishes
exercises.alternateName: Glute stretch with spinal twist, thoracic rotation
exercises.description:
1. Start seated on a mat with your legs crossed. 

2. Bring your right leg up and over your left, and plant your right foot flat on the mat. 

3. Sit up in a tall posture, while using your right hand on the floor behind you for balance. 

4. To deepen the stretch; bring your left hand across your body and gently draw your right knee in towards your chest. 

5. Breathe and relax in this position for the desired amount of time, then repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Hamstring Bridge Iso Hold
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. 

Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your hips into a bridge position, creating a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. 

Hold this position, keeping your core tight and your glutes contracted throughout. Avoid letting your lower back sag.

exercises.name: Handstand pushes
exercises.description: To modify this exercise put more of your legs / knees on the bench or couch. To make this more difficult walk legs up the wall to a handstand position.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.alternateName: toes to bar leg core lift
exercises.description:
1) Hang from a pull-up bar with your legs straight down. 

2) Start by using your abs to bring your knees up until your waist. Pause, and slowly return to the starting position. 

3) Repeat.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise 2
exercises.description: Hanging Leg Raise DONE RIGHT (Common KNEE TUCK Mistakes)

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise DONE RIGHT (Common KNEE TUCK Mistakes)
exercises.description: Hanging Leg Raise DONE RIGHT (Common KNEE TUCK Mistakes)

exercises.name: Hanging Windshield Wiper
exercises.description:
1) While performing this exercise, try to keep your body parallel to the ground. 

2) Rotate your torso so hips move from left to right.

exercises.name: Heel Lift Skips
exercises.description:
1. While travelling forward, kick your heels up towards your butt. 

2. Stay light and springy on the standing leg, and continue to alternate left and right sides.

exercises.name: High Knee Skipping
exercises.description:
1. Drive one of your knees upwards towards your chest.

2. While holding your knee in the upright position, come up onto the toes of the standing leg and do a small hop.

3. Continue by alternating legs, lifting the knee and bouncing on a single leg.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.description:
1) Bring your knees to your hands rather than your hands to your knees.

2) Do the desired number of repetitions

exercises.name: High Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain an active core which means glutes and abdominals on holding neutral spine.

2) Place your hands directly below your shoulders and maintain throughout.

3) Lie face down on a mat with your legs straight, feet together, hands on the floor.

4) Raise your body up into the top of a push-up position, on your hand and toes. Keep your back flat by drawing in your abdominals, and squeezing your glutes. Hold this neutral spine position actively.
